prompt: We use an ad tech platform that receives our website visitors' cookie IDs and browsing data to serve targeted ads. Does this constitute a 'sale' or 'sharing' under CCPA/CPRA? What do we need to do?
assertions: Correctly identifies that passing visitor cookie IDs and browsing data to an ad tech platform for cross-context behavioral advertising constitutes 'sharing' under CPRA even if no money changes hands
Explains that the same arrangement could also be a 'sale' under CCPA if the ad tech platform provides any valuable consideration (e.g., access to inventory, data enrichment)
States the business must add a 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link and must honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals as valid opt-outs

Explains that the ad tech platform must be classified as a 'third party' (not a service provider) unless a contractual arrangement prohibits the platform from using the data for any other purpose — and service provider contracts must be reviewed
Advises on the practical steps: update privacy policy, implement consent management platform (CMP), configure ad tech to stop receiving PI for opted-out consumers
